definition: A chemical bond formed by the sharing of electron pairs between atoms.
The unique ability of carbon to form bonds with other atoms of carbon, giving rise to large molecules.
Different structural forms of carbon in nature, such as diamond, graphite, and fullerenes.
A series of carbon compounds in which the same functional group substitutes for hydrogen in a carbon chain.
Compounds made up of only carbon and hydrogen atoms, categorized into alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.
